paper_title: Holocene Proxy Climate Records from the Arctic

methods.description: Process for compiling a uniform database of Holocene proxy climate records from the Arctic, including both terrestrial and marine environments, to assess spatial and temporal patterns of climate change.

steps.description: Conduct a comprehensive review of the literature to identify proxy climate records published prior to November 2013.
steps.searchable_summary: search and collect proxy climate records from literature

steps.description: Assess the suitability of proxy records and identify key records from multiproxy studies.
steps.searchable_summary: evaluate and select suitable proxy records

steps.description: Gather numerical data and check the accuracy of metadata and data.
steps.searchable_summary: collect and verify numerical data and metadata

steps.description: Subdivide the Arctic into six regions and assign a representative to lead data collection and verification efforts in each region.

methods.description: Systematic scheme for judging the accuracy of 14C-based age models from sedimentary sequences to quantify uncertainty in proxy time series.

steps.description: Recover and input the original 14C data for each sediment-based record.
steps.searchable_summary: retrieve original 14C data for sediment records

steps.description: Update and standardize age models using the recovered 14C data.
Calculate age-model ensembles to enable a statistical approach for quantifying uncertainty in the time domain.
steps.searchable_summary: compute age-model ensembles for uncertainty analysis

methods.description: Management of proxy data and geochronology information for each site, including depth, age, and sample thickness, to ensure accurate climate reconstructions.

steps.description: List proxy data for each site in separate sheets within regional files.
steps.searchable_summary: organize proxy data by site and region

steps.description: Update ages by calibrating 14C ages to calendar age using Calib v5.
Calculate the geochronology accuracy score using supplemental age control data.
